title: More Nonsense Limerick 54
content:
There was an old person of Wilts,
    Who constantly walked upon stilts;
    He wreathed them with lilies 
    And daffy-down-dillies,
    That elegant person of Wilts.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Edward Lear delivers a powerful performance in "More Nonsense Limerick 54"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
